content:
💡 roxum-ide | Dart
🎯 Primary Use Case: Providing a comprehensive and powerful code editing and development environment directly on Android devices.
✨ Key Features: • Mobile-first code editor and mini IDE • Built with Flutter • Editing capabilities • Terminal workflows • Git/GitHub tooling
📖 Summary: Roxum IDE is a mobile-first code editor and mini IDE for Android, built with Flutter. It integrates editing, terminal workflows, Git/GitHub tooling, AI assistance, and runtime downloads into a single application.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ note-gen | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a seamless and intelligent note-taking experience that leverages AI to help users capture, organize, and refine their thoughts and knowledge.
✨ Key Features: • AI-native writing workflow (chat, rewrite, translate, polish) • Image workflow (capture, recognize, describe, host, reuse) • Native Markdown storage (local-first, portable)
📖 Summary: NoteGen is a cross-platform, AI-native Markdown note-taking application designed for capturing ideas and transforming them into structured, readable notes. It offers a rich feature set including AI-powered writing assistance, image processing, local Markdown storage, and flexible synchronization options.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 fingerprint-suite | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Anonymizing web scrapers by injecting realistic browser fingerprints to avoid detection.
✨ Key Features: • Generates configurable, realistic HTTP headers • Generates realistic browser fingerprints affecting JS APIs • Injects browser fingerprints into Playwright/Puppeteer instances • Includes a generative Bayesian network for fingerprint creation • Modular npm packages for separate or combined use
📖 Summary: The fingerprint-suite is a collection of TypeScript tools designed to generate and inject realistic browser fingerprints. It helps anonymize web scraping activities by making automated browsers appear as unique human users, thus bypassing detection.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 tabler-icons | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide web developers with a vast library of versatile and customizable SVG icons for use in their projects.
✨ Key Features: • Over 6000 high-quality SVG icons • MIT-licensed • Designed on a 24x24 grid with a 2px stroke • Available in outline and filled versions • Easy integration via ``, `background-image`, or inline SVG
📖 Summary: Tabler Icons provides a comprehensive collection of over 6000 free, high-quality SVG icons designed for web projects. Each icon is crafted on a 24x24 grid with a 2px stroke, available in both outline and filled versions, and licensed under MIT.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 swaptop | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Monitoring and visualizing swap memory usage on Linux and Windows systems through a text-based user interface.
✨ Key Features: • Real-time animated swap usage graph • List Swap devices with usage per disk / type • Process-level swap consumption tracking • Grouped view by software (aggregate mode) • Multiple color themes
📖 Summary: Swaptop is a real-time swap usage monitor for Linux and Windows systems, featuring a TUI interface that displays swap consumption per process and per software. It provides live-updating graphs, multiple color themes, and unit conversion, all with a lightweight memory footprint.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
